user.name: Sebastian Raschka
user.description:
ML/AI research engineer. Ex stats professor.
Author of "Build a Large Language Model From Scratch" (https://t.co/O8LAAMRzzW) & reasoning (https://t.co/5TueQKx2Fk)

fullText:
Cool new open-weight model by Cohere: a new lightweight 30B open-weight model for agentic coding tasks.

This one builds on Command A+ using the parallel transformer design. Interestingly, even though it's almost half as big, it almost doubles the number of layers.

Also, they say that it's been specifically developed for agentic coding, not just coding. I.e., the evaluation is inside a workflow, not just on a single prompt-to-code-answer task.

For Terminal-Bench, the model has to use a terminal, inspect the environment, run commands, read outputs, etc.

For SWE-Bench the model works on real GitHub-style software issues where it has to understand the repository, find relevant files, make a patch, pass tests, etc.

SciCode and LiveCodeBench are more traditional because they mostly test whether the model can produce correct code for a specified problem. Sure, this still requires reasoning, but it's more like “Implement a numerical routine to compute a scientific quantity from given equations and inputs.” which doesn't require any interaction with the environment, existing files, tests, etc.

The focus on the agentic code benchmarks is probably why it's far ahead of Gemma 4 on those.

Overall, it's pretty competitive although not quite Qwen3.6-level performance.

user.name: Ben Goertzel
user.description: Building Beneficial AGI - CEO @asi_alliance @singularitynet, @true_agi , Interim CEO @Singularity_Fi, @SophiaVerse_AI, Chair @opencog  @HumanityPlus  @iCog_Labs

fullText:
I think there will be a "magic moment" when AGI understands CS and AI dev better than humans do, and can take over its own process of self-improvement, conceptually as well as engineering-wise.

That doesn't mean all of human society will utterly transform all of a sudden though.   The self-improving AGi may well have no desire to micromanage human society, which may be left to adapt to the new situation in its own way -- with AI advice, and very rapidly by historical standards, but not in a single crazy white-hot moment...

I feel like this "magic moment" may well come in the next few years.  On that i agree with @sama @elonmusk and @DarioAmodei ... however I agree with @GaryMarcus that LLMs aren't going to be the core of what gets us there (though I think I feel they can be a bigger part of the story than Gary does, appropriately coupled with other subsystems and embedded in an appropriate cognitive architecture).   

I think the same basic situation and forces that led to the success of LLMs are shortly going to lead to the even greater success of other methods that can synergize with LLMs, and this can lead us to the magic moment in a few years (roughly).....   Which is what I'm pushing toward with Hyperon.   But you know that already ;)

Business users can already use Genie to ask descriptive questions in natural language. Now the same conversational workflow can support predictive analytics too.

By combining Databricks Genie, TabPFN, and Agent Bricks, teams can turn questions like “Which customers are likely to churn?” or “Which promotion is most likely to close this deal?” into predictive answers in seconds.

Instead of relying on specialized ML workflows and dedicated data science teams, the system dynamically assembles governed Lakehouse data and generates predictions within the same conversational experience.
